items.title: Spurs conduct major review to address 'astronomical' injury problems
items.summary: Spurs have carried out a major review of their performance and medical departments, which included an investigation into whether the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium's retractable pitch is contributing to the club's ongoing injury crisis.

items.summary: Tottenham will launch a review focusing on the "unusual" spate of anterior cruciate ligament injuries recently suffered by key players.

items.title: Putellas announces Barcelona exit after 14 years

items.title: Sources: Shaw's City salary among highest in game
items.summary: Manchester City pulled off a surprise U-turn by making WSL Golden Boot striker Khadija 'Bunny' Shaw one of the highest paid players in the world, according to sources, just weeks after she heavily linked with a move away on a free transfer.

items.summary: Real Madrid presidential candidate Enrique Riquelme has claimed he has "two international stars" -- without naming them -- who he'll sign to strengthen the team if he's elected to replace Florentino Pérez.

items.title: Scaloni: Not just Messi recovering for Argentina
items.summary: Lionel Messi's physical condition ahead of the World Cup is commanding the attention of the football world, but the Inter Miami CF star is not the only concern for Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni as he finalizes his tournament roster.

items.title: Gregg Berhalter: Couldn't 'have picked' Sebastian
items.summary: If Gregg Berhalter found any silver lining in being fired as coach of the United States, it was that his departure meant his son, Sebastian, might get a clearer path to starting his own national team journey.
